title: French architects of Iran nuclear deal watch Trump unravel their work
subhed: French diplomats who helped negotiate the 2015 Vienna agreement, aimed to prevent Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons, now watch bitterly as decisions by the Trump administration unravel their work.

title: Many unknowns surround an 'unprecedented and worrying' hantavirus outbreak; WHO seeks to reassure public on pandemic risk
Israel PM says targeted a top Hezbollah commander in Beirut strike
Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
subhed: Law enforcement forces under Hamas's authority are regularly targeted by the Israeli army. Israel cites preventive strikes, targeting police officers who also serve in the armed wing of the Islamist movement. Civil society representatives, on the other hand, accuse Israel of seeking to plunge the territory into chaos.

title: The shifting identities of Ismaël Gamaev, convicted for his relationship with killer of teacher Samuel Paty
subhed: Student Ismaël Gamaev was sentenced for terrorism during the first trial over the murder of teacher Samuel Paty. According to all the professionals who have worked with him, he is a rare case of deradicalization. He tells Le Monde how he became radicalized and how he managed to break away from jihadist ideology.

title: PSG knock out Bayern to lock Champions League final with Arsenal
subhed: With a 1-1 draw, Paris Saint-Germain sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory at Bayern Munich on Wednesday.

title: Ted Turner, founder of CNN, is dead
subhed: The four-time 'Yachtsman of the Year' relished a challenge. When he launched Cable News Network in Atlanta in 1980, few believed the all-news channel would succeed. Also a philanthropist and founder of the United Nations Foundation, Turner died at the age of 87.

subhed: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

title: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
subhed: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

title: Putin's waning popularity worries Kremlin five months ahead of Russian parliamentary elections
subhed: Internet shutdowns have hastened the decline in public trust in the president, amid economic hardships that have affected citizens' daily lives and the war in Ukraine that has only kept dragging on.
